verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass TM protein canonically resident in the ER, where its N-terminal SAM domain faces the ER lumen. Upon ER Ca2+ store depletion, STIM1 translocates to ER-PM junctions and accumulates at the PM with an extracellular-facing N-terminus to activate Orai1 channels. This activation-induced redistribution to the PM is a well-documented, physiologically relevant mechanism. Additionally, some cell types (e.g., vascular smooth muscle) exhibit constitutive PM pools. The protein reaches the outer leaflet via its own TM-mediated mechanism under defined cellular conditions (store-depletion-induced activation).
